definition: China property sector restructuring represents a structural (not cyclical) demand reduction for metals. Housing starts fell from ~15M/year peak to ~7-8M. Demographics (shrinking working-age population, urbanization ~65%) preclude return to prior peaks. A multi-year demand valley between declining property demand and growing green demand stresses mining credits with fixed cost structures.
evidence: China accounts for ~50% of global base metals demand
Housing starts fell from ~15M/year peak to ~7-8M
Demographics preclude return to prior peaks: shrinking working-age population, urbanization near saturation at ~65%
Green demand substitution growing from smaller base creates timing mismatch
open_questions: How fast will green demand substitution close the demand valley?
Which specific mining credits are most exposed?
Will Chinese fiscal stimulus partially offset the structural decline?
